skill.name: meeting-minutes-assistant
skill.description: Organizes meeting recordings or transcripts into structured minutes, action items, and owner follow-ups.
skill.summary: Convert meeting notes into clear minutes with decisions and tasks.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptography pattern on SKILL.md line 4 and a high entropy heuristic on SKILL.md line 1. Both are false positives: the file is a short Markdown instruction document with Chinese metadata and no executable code, cryptographic use, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding points to SKILL.md line 4, which is only a Chinese description of meeting-minute organization. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, key handling, or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line contains only descriptive metadata text. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, weak cipher use, or any crypto-related operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The entropy warning points to SKILL.md line 1 in a 34-line Markdown file. The content is readable front matter and Chinese prose, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payload content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain text Markdown with no encoded blob or hidden executable instructions. The entropy score is plausibly caused by non-ASCII Chinese text and compact metadata.

content.value_statement: Meeting notes are often scattered across transcripts, chat logs, and personal summaries. This skill turns provided meeting content into minutes, decisions, action items, owners, and follow-up tracking.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes provided meeting text into structured minutes.
Extracts decisions, open questions, and follow-up tasks.
Associates action items with owners when names are available.
Creates concise summaries for team sharing.
Highlights items that need human review or missing context.
limitations: It does not include its own audio transcription engine.
It cannot verify facts that are absent from the provided notes.
Owner and deadline accuracy depends on the source transcript.
Important business decisions should be reviewed by a human.

use_cases.title: Prepare team meeting records
use_cases.description: Convert raw meeting notes into minutes with decisions, blockers, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Track client follow-ups
use_cases.description: Extract commitments, owners, and due dates from client meeting transcripts.

use_cases.title: Share project status summaries
use_cases.description: Create a concise recap for teammates who missed a planning or review meeting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic minutes
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a simple meeting summary from notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these meeting notes into structured minutes with agenda, key points, decisions, and action items: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Action tracker
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus on ownership and follow-up work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this transcript and extract every action item. Include owner, deadline, dependency, and confidence when available: [paste transcript].

prompt_templates.title: Executive recap
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a concise update for leadership.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an executive meeting recap from this content. Include outcome, major risks, decisions, and items needing leadership input: [paste content].

prompt_templates.title: Cross-meeting follow-up audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare multiple meetings for unresolved work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these meeting notes and identify repeated topics, unresolved decisions, stale action items, and owners who need follow-up: [paste notes].

output_examples.input: Weekly product sync notes with roadmap discussion, launch risk, and three assigned tasks.
output_examples.output: A structured meeting record with summary, decisions, risks, action items, owners, and review notes.

output_examples.input: Client call transcript about requirements, timeline concerns, and next approval steps.
output_examples.output: A client-ready recap that lists confirmed requirements, open questions, approval owners, and due dates.

output_examples.input: Planning meeting notes from several team members.
output_examples.output: A consolidated summary that removes duplicates and separates decisions from discussion points.

best_practices: Provide speaker names and timestamps when available.
Ask for separate sections for decisions, risks, and tasks.
Review names, dates, and commitments before sharing minutes.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on vague notes when exact commitments matter.
Do not publish minutes without checking sensitive content.
Do not treat inferred owners or deadlines as confirmed facts.

faq.question: Can this skill transcribe audio files?
faq.answer: The skill describes organizing recordings or transcripts, but no transcription code is included. Provide a transcript for best results.

faq.question: Can it identify action item owners?
faq.answer: Yes, it can extract owners when the source text clearly names them.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, the report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for confidential meetings?
faq.answer: Use the same privacy rules as your AI platform. Review confidential content before sharing outputs.

faq.question: Can it create follow-up task lists?
faq.answer: Yes, it can turn meeting content into task lists with owners and deadlines when available.

faq.question: Does it make final business decisions?
faq.answer: No. It helps organize information, but people should confirm decisions and commitments.
